talk_title: African Cancer Atlas (TACA) project and early genomic findings

models.name: TACA cohort
models.genotype_or_key_features: paired germline and somatic whole genomes from African ancestry cancer patients

MOA_summary: The TACA initiative generates paired whole-genome sequencing of African cancer patients to uncover ancestry-specific mutational signatures, driver alterations such as TP53 enrichment, and novel candidate drivers like LEPR and KRTAP10-6, aiming to improve cancer biology understanding and drug discovery for African and global populations.
key_findings: Phase 1 of TACA funded to generate 3,000 genomes from 1,500 African cancer patients across 7 countries.
Median sequencing coverage achieved 41X for germline and 121X for somatic samples, comparable to reference datasets.
Tumor mutational burden in African breast cancer cohort comparable to TCGA, suggesting eligibility for checkpoint blockade.
Enrichment of TP53 mutations and novel driver candidates (e.g., LEPR in TNBC, KRTAP10-6 in luminal) relative to European datasets.
Geographic subtype distribution observed: higher triple‑negative breast cancer prevalence in West Africa versus luminal B in East Africa.

talk_title: Proteomics studies in African cancer cohorts and the African ClinMap multi‑omic platform

speaker.name: Irechen
speaker.affiliation: Council for Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR), South Africa

models.name: Southern African proteomics cohort
models.genotype_or_key_features: 150+ patient tissue samples across multiple cancer types

MOA_summary: Proteomic profiling of African tumor specimens reveals ECM remodeling, altered lipid metabolism, and distinct protein signatures (e.g., decreased CD36 in breast cancer, high LGR1 in pancreatic cancer) that differentiate tumor from normal tissue and suggest ancestry‑specific therapeutic vulnerabilities.
key_findings: Breast cancer proteomics (n=28) identified >2,000 proteins with a five‑fold decrease in CD36 compared to US cohorts.
Machine‑learning classifier achieved AUC 0.96 distinguishing tumor vs normal tissue using proteomic data.
Pancreatic cancer plasma proteomics showed ECM remodeling and inflammatory mediators; LGR1 achieved AUC 0.94 for cancer vs benign.
Prostate cancer tissue proteomics (n>150) uncovered a five‑protein panel linked to neurogenous metastasis.
Gallbladder cancer proteomics identified nine key proteins associated with ECM and smooth muscle contraction loss.

talk_title: Ancestry‑driven genomic and transcriptomic landscape of aggressive endometrial cancer

speaker.name: Nyasha Chambwe
speaker.affiliation: Northwell Health / New York Genome Center, New York, USA

targets.dependency_context: copy number high endometrial cancer, African ancestry
African ancestry endometrial cancer, copy number high

biomarkers.name: CD8 T cell infiltration
biomarkers.finding: Higher African ancestry coefficient associated with lower CD8 T cell infiltration across tumors

models.name: NYC Endometrial Cancer Ancestry Cohort
models.genotype_or_key_features: 71 patients, enriched for Black women, high‑grade non‑endometrioid subtypes, paired tumor/normal WGS and RNA‑seq

MOA_summary: Whole‑genome and transcriptomic profiling of African‑ancestry endometrial cancers reveals ancestry‑specific driver alterations (e.g., enriched MECOM short‑isoform amplifications) and reduced CD8 T‑cell infiltration, indicating distinct molecular and immune landscapes that may affect therapeutic response.
key_findings: African ancestry patients showed lower CD8 T‑cell infiltration after adjusting for tumor purity and subtype.
MECOM short‑isoform focal amplifications were present in ~35% of African ancestry tumors versus ~10% in European ancestry, linked to poorer progression‑free survival in TCGA.
FBXW7 mutations enriched in African ancestry copy‑number‑high endometrial cancers.
Overall fewer clinically actionable mutations (OncoKB) in African ancestry tumors compared with European ancestry.
Ancestry‑specific expression of PLXNA3 and other genes correlated with admixture proportion.
open_questions: Functional impact of MECOM short‑isoform amplification on tumor biology and therapy response.
Mechanisms underlying reduced CD8 infiltration in high‑African‑ancestry tumors.
